texto: Many confuse the United States with the Church or the Constitution with the Bible. They feel that the good of the United States is the same as the good of the Kingdom of God. Some feel that the Constitution of the United States is as infallible as the Bible. However, one with wisdom notice that some things are Kingdom principles and some are not.
explicacao: Citação de Gayle D. Erwin sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Many confuse the United States with the Church or the Constitution with the Bible. They feel that…” articula reflexao, kingdom-of-god, patriotism em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como patriotism.

texto: I still find my corrupt heart longing for tomorrow's bread. I can make a good argument to the Lord about how effective I can be if He would supply me with enough advance funds. It's a little frightening to pray for TODAY's bread. That means I must pray again for tomorrow and believe again for tomorrow. My greedy heart is willing to be corrupted by a little bit of riches so that I see my warehouse full of loaves. I can make a good argument about how God won’t have to be bothered with me every day if He would only advance me about ten years worth of bread.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Gayle D. Erwin sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. A afirmação “I still find my corrupt heart longing for tomorrow's bread. I can make a good argument to the Lor…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a fe, faith, prayer, útil em momentos de prayer e provision.

texto: When the mouth cries, I want to see God, the heart has reached its finest moment. Once we have sought and seen God, all other things have a way of finding us.
